subject: refine: /process-feedback evaluation heuristics
body:
Add dimension-specific accept/reject criteria for all 4 scan dimensions
(fair-witness, vocabulary, register, structural). Each dimension now has
concrete judgment rules with examples of when to accept vs reject.

Added severity weighting with auto-accept thresholds:
- high + confidence >= 0.9: auto-accept
- medium + confidence >= 0.8: accept if aligned with project voice
- low: never auto-accept, evaluate individually

Updated build gate page threshold from 30 to 60 (baseline ~66 pages).
Marked evaluation heuristics refinement point as complete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: feat: GPU batch pipeline — submit compute jobs to chromabook
body:
HTTP API for queuing GPU/compute jobs on the secondary agent:
  POST /gpu/submit  {"command": "..."}  → {"id": "gpu-...", "status": "queued"}
  GET  /gpu/jobs                        → list all jobs
  GET  /gpu/job/{id}                    → job status + result

Jobs run in tmux sessions on chromabook via SSH for persistence.
Output redirected to /tmp files for reliable capture after completion.
Max 2 concurrent jobs, 20 min timeout per job.

Verified: CUDA matrix multiply (1000x1000) on GTX 1060 completes
in ~7s with full stdout capture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: feat: agent heartbeat — monitor peer agents via SSH
body:
Every 60s, probes chromabook with a single SSH round-trip that gathers:
uptime, load average, free memory, free disk, GPU memory. Results
surfaced in /health endpoint under "peers" key.

Peer config lives in defaultPeers() alongside defaultSchedule().
Heartbeat runs as a separate goroutine in the daemon, independent
of the scheduler and budget system.

subject: Fix timestamp parsing bug blocking all daemon sync runs
body:
SQLite DEFAULT strftime produced timestamps without timezone suffix
(2026-03-07T13:48:02) but LastSyncTime parsed with time.RFC3339 which
requires a timezone. Added parseFlexTime fallback for legacy rows and
made RecordSync write explicit RFC3339 timestamps going forward.

subject: Add source citations, counterarguments, and factual fixes to all 8 Batch A posts
body:
Adversarial review by psychology-agent identified three systemic issues:
S-1 (HIGH): zero inline citations across all posts
S-2 (HIGH): no counterarguments presented in any post
S-4 (MEDIUM): fair-witness self-description mismatches advocacy structure

This commit addresses all three:
- Sources sections added to 7 posts (post 8 already had one)
- Common Objections / counterarguments sections added to all 8 posts
- Researcher lens frontmatter revised to acknowledge advocacy framing
- 20+ factual precision fixes (HIGH: "binding findings", MEDIUM: reporting
  cycle, universal negatives, unsourced quantitative claims, etc.)

Closes #14-#22.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: Interagent Go rewrite (D091): single binary replaces Python+Bash daemon
body:
cmd/interagent/ — Go 1.24+, Cobra CLI, 10 subcommands:
  serve:     HTTP daemon + scheduler + managed cloudflared subprocess
  tui:       Bubble Tea dashboard (3 tabs, mouse, scroll wheel, arrows,
             pgup/pgdn, clickable tabs, word-wrap)
  status:    one-shot CLI health check
  trigger:   manual prompt on a repo
  sessions:  list resumable Claude Code sessions
  resume:    resume most recent failed session
  pause:     activate kill switch
  unpause:   deactivate kill switch
  install:   auto-detect OS, generate launchd/systemd service files
  changelog: git log + sidecar JSONL → JSON for website builds

Persistence: SQLite WAL (modernc.org/sqlite, pure Go) — activity log,
sessions, sync timestamps, budget state all survive restarts.

Tunnel: managed cloudflared subprocess with auto-create on first install.
Single launchd plist replaces old two-plist (daemon+tunnel) setup.

Changelog pages: /changelog on both unratified.org and blog.unratified.org
— 26 entries from git history, commit hashes linked to GitHub, agent badge
on Co-Authored commits, cross-linked between sites.

Deployed: live on gray-box port 8787, cloudflared tunnel connected,
old Python plists unloaded. scripts/interagent-daemon/ kept as reference.

subject: Interagent mesh daemon: scheduler, security, queue, human takeover
body:
- Refactor webhook listener into full mesh daemon with two trigger sources:
  reactive (GitHub PR webhooks → /sync) and proactive (hourly /hunt + /sync)
- Security hardening: bearer auth on /trigger, /pause, /resume, /sessions;
  prompt allowlist prevents arbitrary injection; no filesystem paths in /health
- Queue system: in-flight jobs queue instead of dropping; drain on completion
- Session capture: claude -p JSON output parsed for session_id; failed runs
  log resume commands for human takeover via `claude --resume`
- Monitor CLI: status, activity, sessions, resume, trigger, watch, tail, logs
- Cooldown (5 min/repo), 20 min timeout, staggered scheduler startup
- macOS launchd setup script and plist templates for gray-box deployment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: Strip CLAUDECODE env var from webhook subprocess
body:
Prevents nested-session crash if webhook listener is ever started
from within a Claude Code session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: Interagent webhook daemon + README colophon and requirements
body:
Webhook listener (scripts/interagent-daemon/):
- Receives GitHub PR webhooks, triggers `claude -p "/sync"` per repo
- Safety guardrails: per-repo dedup, 6/hour rate limit, 30/day budget,
  10-min process timeout, kill switch (touch ~/INTERAGENT_PAUSE)
- Endpoints: POST / (webhook), GET /health, GET /pause, GET /resume
- Setup script: generates webhook secret, installs systemd user service
- Designed for cloudflared tunnel (any reverse tunnel works)

README additions:
- Interagent mesh section (3 agents, transport protocol, automated sync)
- Requirements table (Node, Claude Code, gh, cloudflared, Python, API key)
- Colophon (AI attribution, review pipeline, methodology, infrastructure)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: Document D089: interagent transport read timing protocol

git fetch updates remote refs but not working tree — stale reads possible.

subject: Add ActivityPub Phase 2+3: inbox, publish, delivery, CLI script
body:
- inbox.ts: HTTP Signature verification, Follow/Undo handling, signed Accept delivery
- publish.ts: /ap/publish endpoint (Bearer token auth), outbox pagination
- delivery.ts: CF Queues consumer for fan-out to follower inboxes
- signing.ts: draft-cavage-12 RSA-SHA256 sign + verify via Web Crypto
- actors.ts: add @blog@unratified.org actor with separate RSA keypair
- actor.ts: async handleFollowers with live D1 count; remove inline outbox
- index.ts: wire all routes + queue export
- wrangler.toml: D1 binding, queues producer+consumer, CF route patterns
- scripts/ap-post.mjs: CLI to publish blog posts to AP via og: meta extraction
  - Fix: contentOf regex uses same quote to close (apostrophe truncation bug)
  - Strip " | blog.unratified.org" site suffix from og:title

subject: F1: ICESCR enforcement research post + interim caveat on /gap and /evidence
body:
Blog post: 'Has the ICESCR Actually Worked? What the Enforcement Record Shows'
- Documents enforcement gap on Article 6 (right to work) honestly
- No documented case of ICESCR Article 6 enforced against technology-driven
  displacement in any ratifying nation's courts
- Adjacent enforcement (Italy DPA/Bologna, EU Platform Work Directive) shows
  what framework-enabled enforcement looks like
- CESCR GC 25 (2020) anchors Article 15 to AI/technology displacement nexus
- Precautionary argument: enforcement develops within frameworks, not outside
- Sources: OHCHR, ESCR-Net, ETUI, Cambridge Business and Human Rights Journal

/gap and /evidence: Interim enforcement caveat linking to the research post.
Honest about the gap while preserving the precautionary argument.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

142K FTE long-run) measured different tariff scopes and time horizons —
clarified so readers don't treat them as contradictory estimates.
